title: eriophyllum confertiflorum var. trifidum
intro: Eriophyllum confertiflorum var. trifidum, also known as Trifid Woolly Sunflower, is a perennial herb in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Eriophyllum confertiflorum var. trifidum include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular choice among herbalists. Eriophyllum confertiflorum var. trifidum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
